description_en: A small rocky body orbiting the sun.
example: Most asteroids are found in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter.

description_en: A region of space having a gravitational field so intense that no matter or radiation can escape.
example: A supermassive black hole lies at the center of our galaxy.

description_en: A group of stars forming a recognizable pattern that is traditionally named after its apparent form.
example: Orion is one of the Most easily recognized constellations in the night sky.

description_en: A celestial body resembling a small planet but lacking certain technical criteria that are required for it to be classed as such.
example: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.

description_en: An obscure of the light from one celestial body by the passage of another between it and the observer or between it and its source of illumination.
example: We wore special glasses to watch the solar eclipse.
note: Shadow cast by a celestial body.

description_en: A system of millions or billions of stars, together with gas and dust, held together by gravitational attraction.
example: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our Solar System.

meaning: chân trời; sự kiện chân trời
description_en: The limit of a person's mental perception, experience, or interest.
example: The event horizon is the boundary around a black hole beyond which no light can escape.

description_en: Situated or occurring between the stars.
example: The Voyager probes are the first human-made objects to enter interstellar space.

description_en: The largest planet in our solar system.
example: Jupiter has a famous Great Red Spot, which is a massive storm.

meaning: ki-lô-pac-sec (đơn vị khoảng cách trong vũ trụ)
description_en: A unit of distance equal to 1,000 parsecs (approximately 3,260 light-years).
example: Distance between galaxies is often measured in kiloparsecs.

description_en: A unit of astronomical distance equivalent to the distance that light travels in one year.
example: The nearest star system, Alpha Centauri, is about 4.3 light-years away.

description_en: A small body of matter from outer space that enters the earth's atmosphere, becoming incandescent as a result of friction and appearing as a streak of light.
example: We saw several meteors during the Perseid meteor shower.

description_en: A cloud of gas and dust in outer space.
example: New stars are born inside massive clouds of gas called nebulae.

description_en: The curved path of a celestial object or spacecraft around a star, planet, or moon.
example: The Moon is in orbit around the Earth.

description_en: A celestial body moving in an elliptical orbit around a star.
example: Mars is known as the 'Red Planet' because of its iron oxide surface.

meaning: vật thể giống sao; chuẩn tinh
description_en: A massive and extremely remote celestial object, emitting exceptionally large amounts of energy.
example: Quasars are some of the brightest objects in the early universe.

description_en: A space exploration vehicle designed to move across the surface of a planet or other celestial body.
example: The Mars rover Curiosity has provided incredible data about the Red Planet.

description_en: A star that suddenly increases greatly in brightness because of a catastrophic explosion that ejects most of its mass.
example: A supernova can briefly outshine an entire galaxy.

description_en: An optical instrument designed to make distant objects appear nearer.
example: Galileo was the first to use a telescope to observe the heavens.

description_en: All existing matter and space considered as a whole.
example: The universe has been expanding ever since the Big Bang.

description_en: A space entirely devoid of matter.
example: Space is a near-perfect vacuum where sound cannot travel.

description_en: A small very dense star that is typically the size of a planet.
example: Our Sun will eventually become a white dwarf at the end of its life.

description_en: An observational branch of astronomy which deals with the study of x-ray emission from celestial objects.
example: X-ray astronomy helps us study extremely hot objects like black holes and neutron stars.

description_en: A star belonging to the main sequence of spectral type G.
example: The Sun is classified as a yellow dwarf star.
note: Category for stars like the Sun.

description_en: The point in the sky or celestial sphere directly above an observer.
example: The sun is at its zenith at noon.

meaning: ngàm kính thiên văn (đo độ cao và phương vị)
description_en: A mounting for a telescope that allows it to move in both altitude (up and down) and azimuth (left and right).
example: This amateur telescope features a simple altazimuth mount.

description_en: The center of mass of two or more bodies that orbit each other.
example: The Moon doesn't orbit the exact center of the Earth, but their common barycenter.

description_en: A celestial object consisting of a nucleus of ice and dust and, when near the sun, a 'tail' of gas and dust particles pointing away from the sun.
example: Halley's Comet is visible from Earth every 75–76 years.

description_en: Non-luminous material that is postulated to exist in space and that could take any of several forms including weakly interacting particles.
example: Dark matter makes up about 85% of the total matter in the universe.

meaning: hành tinh ngoài hệ mặt trời
description_en: A planet which orbits a star outside the solar system.
example: Thousands of exoplanets have been discovered by the Kepler space telescope.

meaning: ngọn lửa mặt trời; bùng phát điện từ
description_en: A sudden brief burst of bright flame or light.
example: A massive solar flare can disrupt radio communications on Earth.

description_en: The force that attracts a body toward the center of the earth.
example: The strength of gravity on the Moon is only one-sixth of that on Earth.

description_en: Relating to the heliosphere, the region of space around the sun.
example: Voyager 2 crossed the heliospheric boundary into interstellar space.
note: Area influenced by the Sun's wind.

description_en: The angle at which something slopes.
example: The Earth's axial inclination is responsible for the seasons.
note: Tilt of an orbit or axis.

meaning: ki-lô-oát (đơn vị năng lượng tàu vũ trụ)
description_en: A unit of power equal to 1,000 watts.
example: The spacecraft provides several kilowatts of power from its solar arrays.

description_en: Of, determined by, or resembling the moon.
example: A lunar eclipse occurs when the Earth passes between the Sun and the Moon.

meaning: thiên thạch; mảnh vỡ từ vũ trụ
description_en: A piece of rock or metal that has fallen to the earth's surface from outer space as a meteor.
example: Scientists can learn about the early solar system by studying meteorites.

description_en: A celestial object of very small radius and very high density, composed predominantly of neutrons.
example: A single teaspoon of material from a neutron star would weigh billions of tons.

description_en: The passage of one celestial body in front of another, thus hiding it from view.
example: The occultation of a star by the moon provides valuable data for astronomers.
note: Hiding of a distant object by a closer one.

description_en: The effect whereby the position or direction of an object appears to differ when viewed from different positions.
example: Astronomers use parallax to calculate the distance to nearby stars.

description_en: A very bright object in the center of some galaxies.
example: Quasars are powered by supermassive black holes.

description_en: The displacement of spectral lines toward longer wavelengths.
example: The redshift of light from distant galaxies shows that the universe is expanding.

description_en: Either of the two times in the year when the sun reaches its highest or lowest point in the sky at noon.
example: The summer solstice is the longest day of the year.

meaning: thuộc về trái đất; hành tinh đá
description_en: Of, on, or relating to the earth.
example: Mars and Venus are known as terrestrial planets because they have solid surfaces.

meaning: vùng tối; bóng tối hoàn toàn
description_en: The fully shaded inner region of a shadow cast by an opaque object.
example: During a total solar eclipse, the observer must be within the Moon's umbra.

description_en: A large constellation on the celestial equator.
example: The Virgo supercluster contains thousands of galaxies, including our own.

description_en: A hypothetical region of space-time and singularity which cannot be entered from the outside.
example: Theoretical physicists speculate that white holes could be the opposite of black holes.

description_en: Production of x-rays from high energy events.
example: Strong x-ray emission is a sign of extreme heat in space.

description_en: The time taken by a planet to make one revolution around the sun.
example: A year on Jupiter is equal to almost 12 Earth years.
note: Orbits take different lengths of time.

description_en: A belt of the heavens within about 8° either side of the ecliptic, divided into twelve equal parts or signs.
example: The zodiac signs are used in astrology but are based on real constellations.
note: Path of the Sun through the sky.
